description:
Advances: specled.branch_guard, specled.api_boundary, specled.implementation_tier, specled.expanded_behavior_tier, specled.use_tier

## Context

q59 built all five realization tiers as standalone modules with unit + integration tests:

- S2 (q59.2): `SpecLedEx.Realization.ApiBoundary.run/1`
- S3b (q59.4): `SpecLedEx.Realization.Implementation.run/4`
- S5a (q59.7): `SpecLedEx.Realization.ExpandedBehavior.run/3`, `SpecLedEx.Realization.Typespecs.run/3`
- S5b (q59.8): `SpecLedEx.Realization.Use.run/3` + `Drift.dedupe/2` extension

Each tier module emits `branch_guard_realization_drift`, `branch_guard_dangling_binding`, and `detector_unavailable` findings. None of them is called from production code:

    grep -rn "ApiBoundary.run\|Implementation.run\|ExpandedBehavior.run\|Typespecs.run\|Use.run\b" lib/
    # (no matches outside lib/specled_ex/realization/ moduledocs)

Every q59.* VERIFY comment attributed "zero findings on subject X" to the pre-existing baseline, not to the tier actually running. The tiers exist but the `mix spec.check` pipeline does not invoke them, so realized_by drift is currently undetected in practice even though every subject now has a `realized_by:` block.

This is a scope-correct gap: no q59 child ticket was scoped to wire the orchestrator. This ticket does that wiring.

## Deliverable

A tier-dispatch step inside `mix spec.check` (most likely inside `SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.run/3`, but see Design decision below) that:

1. Builds a `%SpecLedEx.Compiler.Context{}` via `Context.load/1` â€” no `Mix.env()` / `Mix.Project.config()` reads at tier call sites (hard constraint from q59.2).
2. Enumerates the active `realized_by` bindings per subject by walking the index and applying `SpecLedEx.Realization.EffectiveBinding.for_requirement/2` for each requirement.
3. Dispatches to each tier's `run/*` entrypoint with the bindings it owns (tier key `api_boundary` -> `ApiBoundary.run/1`, `implementation` -> `Implementation.run/4`, `expanded_behavior` -> `ExpandedBehavior.run/3`, `typespecs` -> `Typespecs.run/3`, `use` -> `Use.run/3`).
4. Collects findings, runs `SpecLedEx.Realization.Drift.dedupe/2` to collapse macro-provider fan-out, and appends the result to the check report.
5. Commits new hashes to `SpecLedEx.Realization.HashStore` on success (atomic .tmp + rename semantics â€” already implemented, just invoke).
6. Honors umbrella graceful degrade â€” pass `opts[:umbrella?]` through so tiers emit one `detector_unavailable` per tier rather than raising.
7. Honors `@compile {:debug_info, false}` / `{:no_debug_info, true}` via `expanded_behavior` and `typespecs` tiers' existing `debug_info_stripped` degrade.

## Design decisions open for the picker-upper

These are NOT preordained:

- **Where does the dispatch live?** Options: (a) `SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.run/3` adds a tier step; (b) new module `SpecLedEx.Realization.Orchestrator.run/2` called from BranchCheck; (c) separate Mix task `mix spec.realize.check` that `spec.check` invokes. Option (b) matches the module-per-concern pattern in `lib/specled_ex/realization/`; (a) is the leanest diff; (c) mirrors the `mix spec.triangle` pattern from q59.6.
- **Default-on or opt-in per tier?** A subject with only `api_boundary:` bindings should not silently trigger `implementation` + `expanded_behavior` walks. Simplest rule: run only the tiers explicitly declared in a subject's effective binding map.
- **Config hook?** `SpecLedEx.Config.Realization` exists as a placeholder (from q59.2) â€” may want a `[:realization, :enabled_tiers]` list to let a workspace disable a tier globally without touching spec files.
- **First-run behavior.** On first `mix spec.check` after this lands, no hashes are committed yet. `HashStore.read/2` returning `:not_found` must treat the current hash as the baseline (commit without emitting drift) â€” matches current tier semantics, just verify end-to-end.
- **Failure vs. warning severity.** `branch_guard_realization_drift` severity is configurable per-code via `specled.severity`. Default from q59.1 is `:warning`; confirm this policy flows through without override from the tier step.

## Verification

Tests to pass:

- Existing tier unit tests: `mix test test/specled_ex/realization/` (must continue to pass unchanged).
- New orchestrator test: `test/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator_test.exs` (or integration-level equivalent) that exercises end-to-end dispatch on a fixture: declare a subject with `realized_by: { api_boundary: ["M.f/1"], implementation: ["M.g/2"] }`, commit hashes, mutate M.f/1 head, run `mix spec.check`, assert exactly one `branch_guard_realization_drift` finding with `tier: :api_boundary` in the report.
- Dogfood: after wiring, run `mix spec.check` on this repo. All 31 bindings applied in the follow-on task to q59 should either pass clean or surface real drift that a subsequent spec-edit closes. Do NOT merge with spurious findings; chase them to root cause.

Behaviors to demonstrate:

- A subject with `realized_by.api_boundary: ["M.renamed/1"]` where `M.renamed/1` does not exist -> exactly one `branch_guard_dangling_binding` finding surfaces in the check report (currently tier emits it, but no one reads it).
- A subject with `realized_by.use: ["Provider"]` where the provider's `__using__/1` body changes across N consumers -> Drift.dedupe collapses to one provider-rooted finding, not N per-consumer findings, in the user-visible report.
- Umbrella project (fixture) -> each enabled tier emits exactly one `detector_unavailable` with `reason: :umbrella_unsupported`; no raise.
- Module compiled with `@compile {:debug_info, false}` -> `expanded_behavior` and `typespecs` tiers emit `detector_unavailable` with `reason: :debug_info_stripped`, once per module, and drift is not asserted for that module on those tiers. Other tiers unaffected.

## Allowed touches

- `lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex` (add tier step)
- `lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex` (new, if Option (b) above)
- `lib/specled_ex/config/realization.ex` (extend with enabled_tiers config if chosen)
- `lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex` (minimal â€” only if the task signature needs to expose an opt like `--skip-tiers` for debugging)
- `test/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator_test.exs` (new)
- `test/integration/scenario_tier_dispatch_test.exs` (new, end-to-end smoke)
- `.spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md` (add requirements for tier dispatch semantics, if needed)

## Out of Scope (files)

- `lib/specled_ex/realization/{api_boundary,implementation,closure,expanded_behavior,typespecs,use,drift,binding,canonical,hash_store,effective_binding}.ex` â€” already shipped, do not modify tier internals here. If a tier's `run/*` signature needs to change, file a separate follow-up.
- `lib/specled_ex/compiler/{tracer,xref,manifest,context}.ex` â€” already shipped.
- `lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex` + `lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex` â€” triangulation is a separate read-only diagnostic and stays that way; do NOT fold it into `spec.check` as part of this ticket.
- `lib/specled_ex/coverage/**` â€” per-test coverage capture is orthogonal.

## Out of Scope (intent)

- Do NOT add a `--write` / apply-fix mode to tiers. Drift findings are advisory; authoring fixes is the human's job.
- Do NOT change `Drift.dedupe/2`'s single-function / <=150-LOC contract.
- Do NOT persist use-tier consumer lists in `state.json` (scope cut #27 from q59, still binding).
- Do NOT introduce new Mix globals reads inside tier call paths â€” `Context` must carry everything. The q59.2 AUDIT explicitly flagged this as a hard constraint.
- Do NOT weaken the atomic `HashStore` write semantics.

## Merge gate

- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Existing tier tests pass unchanged
- [ ] New orchestrator + integration tests pass
- [ ] `mix spec.check` on this repo passes (dogfood). If it surfaces drift, root-cause it and fix before merge â€” do not silence with a trailer.
- [ ] `mix spec.check` on a fresh umbrella fixture emits one `detector_unavailable` per enabled tier and does not raise
- [ ] Diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment

## Handoff notes

- The long-term project direction (per user memory, 2026-04-23) is that realized_by + tiered bindings + @tag spec + coverage triangulation supersede the current file-touch co-change guard. This ticket is the pivot that makes that direction load-bearing.
- Sibling ticket: specled_-kox (bug in `spec.suggest_binding`'s module-name camelization for dotted Mix task filenames). Fix that first or in parallel â€” once tiers run, any dangling-binding finding for a mis-camelized Mix task module name will land here.
- The 31 `realized_by` bindings landed on branch `feature/realized-by-triangle-closure` are a good first dogfood input: they declare real contracts against real modules, so a clean tier-check run on that branch is a meaningful smoke signal.
title: Wire realization tiers into mix spec.check
